auditor: Chartered Professional Accountants, Licenced Public Accountants (Kenora, Ontario)
economic_notes: First Nation owns 99.9% of Paagitoon Limited Partnership (janitorial services), accounted for by modified equity method. Ongoing Impact Benefit Agreement with New Gold Inc. providing metal production payments (min $125,000/yr), royalty payments (min $125,000/yr), and annual education payment of $50,000. Contingent asset: expected $83 million flood claim settlement with Canada and Ontario. Committed to upgrading wastewater treatment plant (~$1.196M total cost, $794,000 remaining). COVID-19 impacted program delivery.
notes: Fiscal year is 2021 (year ended March 31, 2021). Auditor firm name not explicitly stated — identified only as Chartered Professional Accountants, Licenced Public Accountants based in Kenora, Ontario. Three subsidiaries fully consolidated: Naotkamegwanning Education Authority, Naotkamegwanning Health and Social Services, Shawendaasowin Child and Family Services. One GBE: Paagitoon Limited Partnership (janitorial services, 99.9% owned), accounted for by modified equity method. Housing units calculated from CMHC mortgages: 9+12+10+10+7+8=56 units. Net debt of $3,818,584 reported on Statement of Changes in Net Debt. Large contingent asset of $83 million flood claim settlement not recorded in financial statements. No schedule of remuneration included in the document. Annual debt service (principal repayments) not separately disclosed; 2022 principal due is $3,711,756. OSR calculated as total revenue $23,747,681 minus total government transfers $17,186,694 = $6,560,987.
